section_aria_label: Why Gangjeong — The Gangjeong Village Story
hook_intro: A nearly 20-year story of peace from Gangjeong Village, Jeju. And the music festival built upon it.
timeline_2007_desc: Gangjeong Village, Seogwipo, Jeju designated as naval base site
timeline_2009_desc: Despite 94% opposition, naval base repackaged as 'tourism port'
timeline_2012_desc: Natural lava rock in UNESCO Biosphere Reserve destroyed by explosives
timeline_2015_desc: Gangjeong Village awarded IPB Sean MacBride Peace Prize — globally recognized
timeline_2016_desc: Base completed after state violence: 700+ detained, 60+ arrested, ₩400M in fines, ₩3.45B in damages claimed
timeline_2018_desc: US nuclear aircraft carrier enters Jeju Naval Base
timeline_2023_desc: Peace music camp launched at Gangjeong Sports Park
timeline_2024_desc: 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know' — 13 tracks
timeline_2025_desc: Naval base upgraded to Fleet Command. US Navy vessels begin regular port calls, integrating Jeju into US military strategy
timeline_2025b_desc: Songs of peace resound again in Gangjeong
timeline_2026_desc: 10 years since base completion, largest ever 3-day festival
story_block1_text: Gureombi Rock, the symbol of Gangjeong. This lava formation, shaped by waves over tens of thousands of years, was blasted in 2012 to make way for the naval base.
story_block2_text: But the memory of people who prayed, sang, and danced on Gureombi never faded. The story of this village — where peace prayers have been offered every morning for nearly 20 years — is the root of this camp.
story_block3_text: Not with military bases, but with music. Not through confrontation, but through solidarity. Gangjeong Peace Music Camp is a festival built on this memory. Because the most beautiful resistance is singing together.
story_block3_alt: Gangjeong Peace Music Camp night festival
solidarity_declaration: From Palestine to the Korean Peninsula — as long as war does not end, our song will not end either
closing_slogan: Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!
paragraph1: In 2007, Gangjeong Village in Seogwipo, Jeju was chosen as the site for a naval base. In a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and habitat of natural monuments, villagers and civil society sustained nearly two decades of resistance to protect their peaceful home.
paragraph2: Gureombi Rock, the symbol of Gangjeong, was destroyed during the base's construction — but the longing for peace that bloomed there never faded. The memory of people who prayed, sang, and danced on Gureombi is the root of this camp.
paragraph3: The Gangjeong Peace Music Camp was built on that memory. Not with military bases but with music, not through confrontation but through solidarity — we hope the sound of peace that began in this small village will reach the world.
hook_image_alt: Gangjeong Village Peace Music Camp scenery in Jeju
